question.text: The Calcutta Hessian Exchange, set up in 1919, started trading in which commodities?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Calcutta Hessian Exchange was set up in 1919 and started trading in raw jute and jute goods. **The Trap:** Cotton was traded at Bombay Cotton Trade Association (1875). Gold and silver are bullion commodities traded on MCX. Rice and wheat are agricultural but not specific to Calcutta Hessian Exchange. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers the history of Indian exchanges. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Early Indian commodity exchanges were regional and commodity-specific — the transition to multi-commodity national exchanges (MCX, NCDEX) was a significant market evolution.
